OBJECTION HEARING CALENDAR
Off Calendar Jun-20-2018 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Service For Lack Of Proper Service; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Thereof
Off Calendar May-03-2018 Denied
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Marshall Way, Esq. 916-379-3450 of Dreyer Babich Buccola Wood & Campora LLP for Plaintiff Martha Gilmore through Court Call.
Stefanie Desalernos, Esq. 562-216-5000 of Clinton & Clinton for Defendant OMAR DAHMASH through Court Call.

RULING - A R G U E D. Defendant argues Defendant was not served. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

SPECIALLY APPEARING DEFENDANT OMAR DAHMASH'S Motion To Quash Service For Lack Of Proper Service.

Defendant Omar Dahmash's motion to quash service is DENIED. Registered process server Feely declares under penalty of perjury that he personally served Dahmash in Belmont, CA on December 17, 2017. Evidence Code 647 provides: "The return of a process server registered pursuant to'the Business and Professions Code'establishes a presumption'of the facts stated in the return." Dahmash fails to rebut the presumption; he merely asserts that he resided in San Diego on December 17, not that he was not served or that a different person was served. (See American Exp. Centurian Bank v. Zara (2011) 199 Cal.App.4th 383 (granting motion to quash where defendant declared he was not served, and description in process servers' declaration of person served did not match defendant). (as fully reported in the reporter's transcript)

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr., Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Christine Jordan, CSR#12262, cjordan@sftc.org, Reported. (D302/RBU)
